name: Regulating Act 1773
summary: The first British statute to impose parliamentary regulation on the East India Company. It created the office of Governor-General of Bengal with a supervisory Council, established a Supreme Court at Calcutta, and began transforming the Company from a trading body into an instrument of British imperial governance.
key_provisions: Governor-General of Bengal created; Warren Hastings appointed first incumbent
Council of Four — majority vote rules; Governor-General has casting vote
Supreme Court at Calcutta — three judges appointed by the Crown
Governors of Bombay and Madras subordinated to Bengal in war and peace
Company servants prohibited from receiving presents or engaging in private trade
Company required to lay all correspondence before the Secretary of State
demise: Substantially amended and superseded by Pitt's India Act 1784.
